hero_badge: v5.6.0 + TUI v9.13.0 · Open source · 21 MCP tools
hero_subtitle:
The cognitive exoskeleton for humans and AI agents.
Think. Simulate. Prove. Discover.
hero_tags: Z₃³ · 27 STATES · 6 OPERATORS · 51 SOURCES · 38 ENGINES · 21 MCP · O₀→O₁→O₂ · 23 MPs
hero_meta: AGPL-3.0 · BYOK · Multi-Prompt Router · Terminal-First · OpenRouter · XAI · Mistral · Moonshot · DeepSeek · Liquid · NVIDIA · YandexGPT · Ollama · LM Studio · MLX
fp_title: Agents Can Talk. But Can They Think?
fp_desc: AI agents have language. They lack cognition. c4reqber is the scientific body for the agentic mind.
fp_no_sim_desc: LLMs generate hypotheses but cannot test them. No physics engine, no statistical validation, no ground truth.
fp_no_verify_desc: ChatGPT "proves" theorems by generating convincing text. Formal verification requires Lean4, Coq, Dafny.
fp_no_cog_desc: Agents cannot recognize their own observational biases or identify gaps in their reasoning. c4reqber uses O₀→O₁ observer shifts to surface blind spots automatically.
fp_no_rigor_desc: Falsifiability, real citations, reproducibility — the foundation of science. Absent from most agent frameworks.
fp_conclusion: c4reqber solves all four. Formal verification + 38 simulation engine bridges + Z₃³ cognitive topology + real citations + O₀→O₁→O₂ meta-cognitive observer.
features_desc: Not features. Cognitive organs. Each module is a real implementation.
feat_c4_desc: 27 discrete cognitive states across Time, Scale, and Agency dimensions. Every query is mapped, tracked, and navigable.
feat_verify_desc: Lean4, Coq, Dafny, Agda, Z3/Hoare, Haskell, CVC5, TLA+, Alloy (9 real bridges).
feat_sim_desc: Molecular dynamics (GROMACS, OpenMM), quantum (PySCF), CFD (OpenFOAM), neuro (NEURON), and more.
feat_kg_desc: arXiv, PubMed, Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex, Europe PMC, Crossref, Zenodo, ClinicalTrials.gov, GBIF, NASA Earthdata, Materials Project, Kaggle, OpenFDA, ORCID, and 30 more. MultiSourceSearcher with circuit breaker and semantic dedup.
feat_pub_desc: LaTeX + BibTeX + Markdown + JSON + HTML exports. Citation verifier with hallucination detection. Ready for arXiv, bioRxiv, or peer review.
feat_mcp_desc: Expose full C4REQBER capability to AI agents. JSON Schema compliant. stdio JSON-RPC.
c4_desc: Discrete group-theoretic cognitive space. Not neural networks. Not symbolic rules. Mathematical topology.
comp_desc: Not an LLM wrapper. A cognitive operating system.
pt_desc: 7 phases. Each gets its own LLM model, plugins, and cognitive strategy.
pt_swap: Models are fully configurable per phase — swap them in your config.
qs_academic_hint: Academic tip: Run with --verify flag to auto-invoke Lean4/Coq for theorem-checking.
roadmap_desc: Continuous delivery of simulation engines, AI integrations, and verification backends.

engines_desc: 5 internal GPU engines + 33 open-source bridges. From molecular dynamics to climate modeling.
engines_note: P1 = Python-native open-source bridge · 5 Built-in GPU engines · Full list in src/simulations/
sysreq_desc: macOS and Linux are tested and supported. Windows is not tested. External engines require separate installation.
pip_scope_note: Note: pip install c4reqber installs the Python package + CLI only. External simulation engines (GROMACS, OpenFOAM, etc.) require separate installation per engine. See docs/setup/gpu.html.
price_desc: From $0.002 to $0.11 per full pipeline run. Configure per phase.
usecases_desc: From hard science to social sciences — c4reqber adapts to your domain.
uc_hard_desc: Physics, chemistry, biology, materials. TRIZ + 38 simulation engine bridges + formal verification.
uc_psych_desc: CBT meta-analysis, organizational behavior, political psychology. Causal inference + agent models.
uc_clinical_desc: RCT design, biomarkers, drug-drug interactions. PubMed + ClinicalTrials.gov + STRING.
uc_eco_desc: Biodiversity, climate modeling, ocean dynamics. GBIF + NASA Earthdata + WRF.
uc_law_desc: Legal reasoning, precedent analysis, regulatory impact. Multi-jurisdiction + temporal reasoning.
cta_sub: One command. One dissertation. One paradigm shift.
cta_license: AGPL-3.0 · BYOK · No data retention · Self-hostable
badge_formal_theory: Formal Theory Behind the Cognitive Engine
trust_desc: Type-checked baseline. Battle-tested core. Transparent limitations.
trust_feat_sanitize_desc: Every LLM input is sanitized. Nonce delimiters, LaTeX escaping, HTML entity decoding. Fail-closed.
trust_feat_sandbox_desc: Simulation engines run in isolated environments. No shell injection. Path traversal blocked. Symlink guards.
trust_feat_proofs_desc: Lean4, Coq, Dafny, Agda, Z3 — every formal proof is machine-checked. No hand-waving.
trust_feat_audit_desc: Multi-agent automated audits, static analysis (mypy, Bandit), and community security reviews.
sources_desc: No hallucinated references. Every citation is verified against live databases with DOI.
theory_why_p1: Modern AI generates text. It does not think. c4reqber is built on a mathematical theory of cognition that treats thinking as navigation through a discrete state space — just like GPS navigates through coordinates.
theory_why_p2: Every research problem gets a cognitive coordinate F⟨Time, Scale, Agency⟩. The system then computes the shortest path to a solution state, selects transformation operators, and validates each step against formal theorems. This is not metaphor — theorems are verified in ~3,600 lines of Agda: gitlab.com/cognitive-functors/Adaptive-topology/-/tree/main/formal-proofs.
theory_ucos_desc: UCOS integrates every component into a single analysis framework. It maps how a query flows from process to topology and back.
theory_ucos_flow: A query enters at Layer 1 (Process), gets profiled by Layer 2 (23 core MPs), localized in Layer 4 (C4 coordinates), and transformed by Layer 3 (QZRF operators) until it reaches the target state.
theory_c4_title: C4 / ℤ₃³ — The Cognitive Coordinate System
theory_c4_desc: Cognition operates in a discrete 3D space: Time × Scale × Agency, each with 3 values. Total: 27 states. This is the minimal complete structure for general intelligence — verified by group theory and category theory.
theory_qzrf_desc: QZRF (Quantum Zone of Recursive Fluctuations) is the "pulse" between topological structure (C4) and static content (metaprograms). Its 14 operators aggregate 72 Matrix Dream micro-patterns into 5 phase-based transformation groups. Each operator targets a specific C4 state.
theory_cdi_desc: Breakthroughs do not come from incremental steps. They require controlled traversal of distant cognitive states — temporarily occupying incompatible positions that break current assumptions. CDI is a 6-step algorithm that exploits Theorem 11 (diameter ≤ 6) to guarantee bounded search.
theory_cdi_einstein: Einstein's insights represent maximally distant conceptual leaps — exactly what C4 predicts for revolutionary breakthroughs. The search complexity is O(1) because the state space is bounded.
theory_observer_desc: Cognition is not just about states — it is about who observes them. The Observer Position adds a meta-cognitive dimension modeled with modal logic ◯ₙ. Each level sees a different subset of the 27-state space.
theory_observer_use: c4reqber uses observer shifts during pipeline execution: O₀ for normal processing, O₁ for self-diagnostic overlay (~10–20% overhead), O₂ for global architecture restructuring when stuck.
theory_meta_desc: C4-META extends C4 with 6 theorems about the observer as an invariant isomorphism between cognitive universes. The framework is formalized in ~3,600 lines of Agda across 6 modules (safe mode, without K).
theory_phi_desc: A theoretical construct from C4-META: the convergence point where Present, Abstract, and Other align. The geometry of the 27-state space creates a natural basin around Φ, with 18 states within reach and 9 distant states. This is explored in the compassion-convergence.agda proof module.
theory_proofs_desc: The core theorems are verified in multiple proof assistants. T1–T11 (C4 properties) and T12–T17 (categorical necessity, observer theorems) are machine-checked.
theory_why3_desc: Three gradations are the minimum for nonlinear interpolation between polar opposites (past/future, concrete/meta, self/system). Binary is too coarse; quaternary wastes states. The 27-state space has been validated against 18 historical scientist discovery paths.
theory_mp_desc: MPRotationStep creates 3 agent profiles with different MP combinations. Each generates key_insights and blind_spots. SynthesisStep integrates them to eliminate cognitive blind spots through perceptual filter rotation.

disc_sub: Six full blast turbo runs — literature search, gap mining, simulation, and gated dissertation prose. Real pipeline artifacts, not marketing copy.
disc_epistemic: Epistemic status: These are research proposals (untested hypotheses + computational pre-screening). They are not peer-reviewed dissertations. Every file includes an explicit disclaimer. Validate citations and claims before any use.

disc_card_amr_desc: Engineered phage + CRISPR hypotheses to restore antibiotic efficacy against resistant pathogens.
disc_card_soil_desc: Regenerative agriculture hypotheses for desertification reversal and durable soil carbon pools.
disc_card_plastic_desc: Topic-routed biogeochemistry sim; microbial/enzyme remediation hypotheses v2.

cl_sub: In plain language — no jargon required.
cl_what_desc: A terminal-first research assistant. You ask a scientific question — c4reqber searches 51 literature sources, runs simulations, checks proofs, and writes a gated research proposal. Not a chatbot. A pipeline.
cl_install_desc: One command. Python 3.11+. Bring your own API key (OpenRouter, Mistral, etc.). Optional GPU for heavy sims.
cl_use_desc: blast solve "your question" for a one-shot run. blast turbo for paradigm proposals. blast tui for the visual cockpit. blast serve --mcp for AI agents.

df_s1_desc: Strip HTML, normalize unicode, block injection

aud_1_html:
1. PhD Students and Early-Career Researchers (Year 1–3)

Problem: "I know I'm interested in X, but I don't understand what's already been done and where the gaps are."

What c4reqber provides:

  • Literature review in 2 minutes instead of 2 weeks — 51 sources: arXiv, PubMed, Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex, Europe PMC, Crossref, Zenodo, and more
  • Structured gaps with potential assessment
  • Formulation of 3–5 competing hypotheses with falsifiable predictions
  • List of control experiments (DoE design)

Honesty: This is a starting point, not a final review. Hypotheses require expert verification — LLMs can hallucinate mechanisms.
aud_2_html:
2. Interdisciplinary Researchers

Problem: "I'm a biologist, but my problem requires physics/math/computer science. I don't know which tools from neighboring disciplines are applicable."

What c4reqber provides:

  • TRIZ analysis: 40 inventive principles applied to your problem
  • Isomorphisms: "Here are 3 similar problems from chemistry, solved with methods you don't know as a biologist"
  • C4 navigation across 27 cognitive states — structures chaotic thinking
  • Transfer pipeline: automatic analogy search between domains

Honesty: Works best when disciplines are close (biochemistry → biophysics). Quality drops for distant bridges (quantum mechanics → sociology).
aud_3_html:
3. Engineers and R&D Managers

Problem: "We need to improve a product/process, but the team is stuck in a local optimum."

What c4reqber provides:

  • TRIZ contradictions: "Increase speed without increasing energy consumption"
  • Patent landscape analysis (USPTO, OpenReview, Google Patents)
  • Structured "paradoxes" with proposed solutions
  • Simulations: CFD (OpenFOAM, FEniCSx), molecular dynamics (GROMACS, LAMMPS), multi-physics (MuJoCo, PyBullet)
aud_4_html:
4. Psychologists and Social Scientists

Problem: "My field is hard to formalize. Can AI even help study human behavior, therapy, or social phenomena?"

What c4reqber provides:

  • Literature search across psychological databases: PubMed (psychiatry), ClinicalTrials.gov, OpenReview, Harvard Dataverse, Kaggle
  • Causal inference (DoWhy, EconML, gCastle): "Does X affect Y?" with confounder control — critical for A/B tests in therapy
  • Agent models (Mesa, SimPy): modeling group behavior, norm diffusion, herding effects
  • Contradiction mining: finding contradictory claims in literature
  • TRIZ for social systems: 40 principles applicable to organizational problems

Honesty: c4reqber does not diagnose patients and does not replace a therapist. In social sciences, quality depends on data quality — if your niche has few peer-reviewed articles, LLMs will hallucinate. But for literature review, hypothesis structuring, and causal modeling, it is a powerful tool.
aud_5_html:
5. Clinical Researchers / Medical Doctors

Problem: "Need to design an RCT, find biomarkers, understand drug mechanism of action."

What c4reqber provides:

  • PubMed + Europe PMC + ClinicalTrials.gov + STRING + ChEMBL + PubChem
  • Causal inference for observational data: "Which factor actually causes the side effect?"
  • Simulations: pharmacokinetics (COPASI, Tellurium), PBPK models
  • Network pharmacology: STRING + Cytoscape-compatible output
aud_6_html:
6. Ecologists, Climatologists, Geographers

Problem: "Complex multi-factor systems, long time series, uncertainty in models."

What c4reqber provides:

  • GBIF (biodiversity), NASA Earthdata (satellite), USGS (geology), CERN (physics)
  • Bayesian models (PyMC, Stan): uncertainty quantification
  • Agent models: ecosystem simulations (predator-prey, invasive species)
  • Simulations: WRF (weather), FEniCSx (fluid dynamics for ocean modeling)
aud_7_html:
7. Data Scientists and ML Researchers

Problem: "Feature engineering, model selection, interpretation, causal claims from correlation data."

What c4reqber provides:

  • Kaggle + UCI ML Repository + Harvard Dataverse: dataset search
  • Causal inference: "Is this feature actually causal or just correlated?"
  • AutoML: automatic model enumeration (autoresearch layer)
  • Bayesian models: uncertainty quantification, model comparison (BMA)
  • Simulations: synthetic data generation for edge cases
aud_8_html:
8. Formal Verifiers / Theorists

Problem: "I have an idea, but I want to check it for contradictions formally."

What c4reqber provides:

  • Auto-generation of formal specification from natural language (Lean4, Coq, Dafny, Z3, Hoare)
  • Iterative compilation error correction (LLM → compile → error → fix loop)
  • Export proofs to .lean, .v, .smt2
  • MathDetector: automatic categorization of mathematical statements

Honesty: Auto-generation from natural language is not yet fully implemented. Works best if you explicitly provide the theorem statement.
aud_compare_html:
Honest Comparison with Analogues

ToolWhat it doesWhere c4reqber is betterWhere c4reqber is worse
Elicit / ConsensusLiterature search + summarizationStructured hypothesis with predictions, not just summaryUX is simpler in Elicit
SciSpaceReading papers with AI assistantCross-paper synthesis + contradiction detection + 51 sourcesNo inline PDF reader
ChatGPT / ClaudeGeneric Q&ADomain-aware simulation (38 engine bridges), formal verification, TRIZ, causal inference, structured pipelineGeneric Q&A is sometimes more accurate for simple questions
MATLAB / MathematicaSimulations and computationHypothesis generation + literature search. MATLAB does neitherMATLAB has decades of industrial-grade solver refinement; our 38 engine bridges span broader scientific domains at research-grade fidelity
aud_hero_sub: Who is c4reqber for? Honest assessment by research domain and TRL level.
aud_not_help_html:
Who It Does NOT Help (and Why)

WhoWhy it won't help
Experimenters who need dataThe system does not run experiments. It will say "you need to measure X" but won't measure X for you
Ultra-niche researchersIf your niche has <100 papers in 10 years, LLMs aren't trained on this data. Hypotheses will be generic or hallucinated
Those expecting guaranteed publishable resultsLLM generation is a creative trigger, not peer-reviewed science
Those who don't want to thinkThe system requires critical evaluation of output. Blind trust = noise
Practicing clinicians (treating patients)c4reqber is a research tool, not a diagnostic system. Do not use for diagnosis or treatment without peer review
aud_summary_html:
Summary

c4reqber is a research assistant at the level of "a very smart PhD student who read 200 papers over the weekend and proposed 5 hypotheses."

  • Does not replace: experiments, expertise, peer review, scientist intuition, therapeutic care
  • Accelerates: literature review, hypothesis formulation, structuring thinking, contradiction search, causal modeling, interdisciplinary transfer

If you expect c4reqber to "discover a new law of physics" — you will be disappointed.
If you expect c4reqber to "systematize your chaos of thoughts and point where to look next" — you will get exactly that.
aud_trl_html:
LevelMeaningCan c4reqber help?
TRL 1 — Basic principlesIdentify fundamental principlesYes. C4 navigation + 51 source literature search finds theory gaps
TRL 2 — Concept formulationFormulate a hypothesisYes. LLM generation with falsifiable predictions is a core feature
TRL 3 — Proof of conceptAnalytical or simulation proofPartially. 38 simulation engine bridges show direction, but these are toy models
TRL 4 — Lab validationLaboratory validationNo. The system has no hands, eyes, or reagents
TRL 5–9Pilot, field validation, commercializationNo. Out of scope

Conclusion: c4reqber operates at TRL 1–3. It helps move from "I have a problem" to "I have a structured hypothesis with mechanism, predictions, and literature list." Everything beyond that requires a human with a lab, data, or mathematical apparatus.

mcpdoc_tools_intro: c4reqber exposes 21 MCP tools for cognitive operations (blast_* + c4_* families).
meta_desc_api: c4reqber API reference: blast commands, REST endpoints, Python SDK, 21 MCP tools.
meta_desc_arch: C4REQBER system architecture: 51 knowledge sources, 38 simulation engine bridges, 32 adapters, Z₃³ topology, O₀→O₁→O₂ observer, 23 core metaprograms.
meta_desc_theory: C4-META Theory — ℤ₃³ cognitive topology, 27 states, 14 QZRF operators, UCOS 4-layer architecture, CDI algorithm, and Agda-verified formal proofs.
meta_desc_gpu: Configure GPU acceleration for c4reqber. NVIDIA CUDA, Apple Metal, AMD ROCm, and cloud GPU via vast.ai.
meta_desc_mcp: Configure c4reqber MCP tools for Claude, Cursor, Cline, and other MCP-compatible agents.
meta_desc_aud: Who is c4reqber for? PhD students, interdisciplinary researchers, engineers, psychologists, and more.
th_cube_desc: 27 discrete states across Time, Scale, and Agency. Click any state to explore. Drag to rotate.
th_hero_sub:
Discrete group-theoretic cognitive space.
27 states across 3 axes — Time, Scale, Agency.
Mathematical topology with Agda-verified proofs.
Not neural networks. Not symbolic rules.
A coordinate system for general intelligence.
th_js_desc_000: Personal concrete memories. Historical experiments you ran.
th_js_desc_001: Concrete memories of others. Historical data you observed.
th_js_desc_002: Concrete system history. Raw experimental logs.
th_js_desc_010: Personal abstract reflection. "What did I learn?"
th_js_desc_011: Abstract analysis of others. Typical academic review.
th_js_desc_012: Abstract system patterns. Meta-analysis of literature.
th_js_desc_021: Meta-analysis of others. Theory of mind.
th_js_desc_100: Present concrete self. Current experiment setup.
th_js_desc_101: Present concrete other. Observing a colleague.
th_js_desc_110: Present abstract self. "What am I doing now?"
th_js_desc_111: Present abstract other. Standard academic analysis.
th_js_desc_120: Present meta self. Mindfulness in research.
th_js_desc_122: Present meta system. Active paradigm construction.
th_js_desc_200: Future concrete self. Personal research plan.
th_js_desc_201: Future concrete other. Predicting a colleague's results.
th_js_desc_210: Future abstract self. "Where is my field going?"
th_js_desc_211: Future abstract other. Forecasting a competitor.
th_js_desc_220: Future meta self. Personal paradigm shift.
th_js_desc_221: Future meta other. Predicting scientific revolutions.
th_js_desc_222: Future meta system. Paradigm-shifting vision. The Einstein state.
meta_desc_keys: Register free API keys for OpenRouter, NCBI, CORE, Tavily, and 20+ scientific knowledge sources. Step-by-step BYOK guide for c4reqber.
keys_hero_sub: BYOK setup for LLM providers and 51 knowledge sources. Verified July 2026.
keys_intro: c4reqber is free (AGPL-3.0) — you only pay upstream APIs. Minimum: one OpenRouter key. Add science keys to unlock full MultiSourceSearcher throughput.
doc_card_keys_desc: Register OpenRouter, NCBI, CORE, Tavily, and 20+ free science APIs. BYOK step-by-step.
gs_next_keys: Register API keys — OpenRouter + scientific knowledge sources
keys_required_html:
ServiceEnvSign upWaitCost
OpenRouterOPENROUTER_API_KEYopenrouter.ai/keysConfirm emailFree :free models

Create key → copy sk-or-... (shown once). No credit card for free tier.

keys_pending_html:
• Semantic Scholar — SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY — request form — 1–2 business days
  • BASE (Bielefeld) — BASE_API_KEY — application + use case — few days
  • DataCite — DATACITE_API_KEY — manual approval
  • OMIM — OMIM_API_KEY — manual approval
  • DrugBank — paid only; stub when empty
keys_nokey_html:
arXiv, PubMed (slow without NCBI key), Crossref (CROSSREF_MAILTO), Europe PMC, DOAJ, Zenodo, PubChem, ChEMBL, ClinicalTrials.gov, Inspire-HEP, DBLP, OpenReview, CyberLeninka, Math-Net.Ru, and more.

See src/knowledge/config.py → SOURCE_REGISTRY for the full list.
keys_setup_note: Copy .env.example → .env. Never commit secrets. Verify: python3 tools/check_keys.py
keys_full_html:
This page is the quick reference. The full bilingual guide (EN + RU, social publishing, troubleshooting) lives in the GitLab repo:

📄 docs/API_KEYS.md on GitLab →

Also: .env.example in the repository root lists every variable.
